activities.name: Cherry Blossoms at Gardens of the World
activities.description: Wander beneath clouds of pink and white cherry blossom in the Japanese garden at Gärten der Welt, one of Berlin's loveliest springtime sights.
activities.seasonal_notes: Blossoms typically peak from late March to mid-April, a gift of the post-reunification cherry trees planted along the former Wall.
activities.booking_tips: Go on a weekday morning to beat the crowds, and buy the park ticket online to skip the line.

activities.name: Kite-flying and picnics on Tempelhofer Feld
activities.description: Spread out on the runways of the former Tempelhof airport—now a vast open park—for kite-flying, cycling, urban gardening, and picnics.
activities.seasonal_notes: Spring's first warm, breezy days are ideal here before the summer crowds arrive; the open field catches the wind beautifully.
activities.booking_tips: Bring your own kite and snacks; the nearest entrances are by U-Bahn Boddinstraße and S-Bahn Tempelhof.

activities.name: MaerzMusik Contemporary Music Festival
activities.description: A boundary-pushing festival of new and experimental music staged at Haus der Berliner Festspiele, Radialsystem, and venues across the city.
activities.seasonal_notes: Held in late March (20–29 March in 2026), MaerzMusik is one of Germany's most important festivals for Neue Musik.
activities.booking_tips: Programmes and tickets are released by the Berliner Festspiele; book ahead for marquee premieres.

activities.name: Gallery Weekend Berlin
activities.description: The city's contemporary-art galleries throw open their doors for a single coordinated weekend of openings, talks, and special exhibitions.
activities.seasonal_notes: Held the first weekend of May (1–3 May in 2026), it's the highlight of Berlin's spring art calendar with dozens of galleries taking part.
activities.booking_tips: Pick up a route map online and cluster your visits by neighbourhood—Mitte, Potsdamer Straße, and Charlottenburg.

activities.name: Spree River Cruise
activities.description: Glide past Museum Island, the Reichstag, and the government quarter on a relaxing cruise along the Spree as the city greens up.
activities.seasonal_notes: Mild spring temperatures and budding riverbanks make this an ideal early-season way to see the city from the water.
activities.booking_tips: Departures cluster around Friedrichstraße and Nikolaiviertel; look for combo tickets with other attractions.

activities.name: 1. Mai Street Festivals in Kreuzberg
activities.description: May Day in Kreuzberg is a Berlin institution—MyFest-style street parties, open-air concerts, and food stalls fill the streets around Oranienplatz.
activities.seasonal_notes: 1 May is a public holiday with a long protest-and-party tradition in Kreuzberg; expect big, lively crowds and a heavy police presence after dark.
activities.booking_tips: Come during the day for the family-friendly, festival side; travel by U-Bahn as streets are closed to traffic.

activities.name: Karneval der Kulturen
activities.description: Berlin's Carnival of Cultures fills Kreuzberg with a vast multicultural street parade, world-music stages, and food from across the globe.
activities.seasonal_notes: Held over the Pentecost (Whitsun) weekend in late May (22–25 May in 2026, with the parade on 24 May), drawing over half a million visitors.
activities.booking_tips: The street festival is at Blücherplatz; arrive early along the parade route for a good viewing spot.

activities.name: Berlin Half Marathon
activities.description: Run or cheer the GENERALI Berlin Half Marathon, a fast, flat course past the Brandenburg Gate that draws tens of thousands of runners.
activities.seasonal_notes: Held in early April, the cool spring air makes for ideal running conditions through the city centre.
activities.booking_tips: Register months in advance if you want to run; spectating along Unter den Linden is free.

activities.name: Christopher Street Day (Berlin Pride)
activities.description: Berlin's huge Pride celebration, with a colourful parade from the city centre to the Brandenburg Gate and open-air parties across town.
activities.seasonal_notes: The main CSD parade falls in late July (25 July in 2026), the peak of a summer-long Pride season in one of Europe's most LGBTQ-friendly cities.
activities.booking_tips: Bring water and sun protection for the long parade; the after-parties around Nollendorfplatz run late.

activities.name: Fête de la Musique
activities.description: On the summer solstice, hundreds of free open-air stages spring up across Berlin's neighbourhoods, turning the whole city into a music festival.
activities.seasonal_notes: Always held on 21 June, the longest day of the year, marking the start of Berlin's outdoor festival season; around 300 stages in 2026.
activities.booking_tips: No tickets needed—pick a few districts and wander between stages as the evening unfolds.

activities.name: Lake Day at Strandbad Wannsee
activities.description: Spend a hot afternoon at Berlin's grand 1920s lido on the Wannsee, with a long sandy beach, swimming, and rentable wicker beach baskets.
activities.seasonal_notes: Berlin's lakes—Wannsee and Müggelsee chief among them—are the heart of summer life when temperatures climb.
activities.booking_tips: Take the S-Bahn to avoid parking, and arrive early on heatwave weekends as it fills up fast.

activities.name: Badeschiff Floating Pool
activities.description: Swim in a pool moored right in the Spree at the Badeschiff, with deckchairs, a bar, and skyline views; by night it becomes a club.
activities.seasonal_notes: One of Berlin's most distinctive summer spots, open through the warm months in Alt-Treptow.
activities.booking_tips: Arrive early on hot days to secure a deckchair; stay on for the evening DJ sessions.

activities.name: Open-Air Cinema at Freiluftkino Hasenheide
activities.description: Watch films under the stars in Volkspark Hasenheide, with a mix of blockbusters, indie titles, and English-language screenings.
activities.seasonal_notes: Warm summer evenings and late sunsets make Berlin's many Freiluftkinos a beloved seasonal ritual.
activities.booking_tips: Bring a cushion or blanket and check the programme for OmU (subtitled) screenings.

activities.name: Biergarten Afternoon at Prater Garten
activities.description: Relax under chestnut trees at Berlin's oldest beer garden, pouring its own brew in Prenzlauer Berg since 1837.
activities.seasonal_notes: Beer gardens spill across the city all summer; Prater is the classic, self-service and family-friendly.
activities.booking_tips: It's first-come seating—grab a bench, order at the counter, and stay for the long golden evening.

activities.name: Mauerpark Flea Market & Bearpit Karaoke
activities.description: Hunt for vintage finds and street food at the Mauerpark flea market, then join the crowd for the legendary outdoor 'Bearpit Karaoke'.
activities.seasonal_notes: Summer Sundays are when Mauerpark is at its liveliest, with the amphitheatre karaoke in full swing.
activities.booking_tips: Go early for the market, then stake out a seat in the amphitheatre by early afternoon.

activities.name: Tiergarten Cycling
activities.description: Rent a bike and ride the shaded paths of the Tiergarten, Berlin's great central park, past monuments and the Spree.
activities.seasonal_notes: Cool summer mornings are perfect for cycling before the day heats up.
activities.booking_tips: Use a bike-share app or a nearby rental shop; cafés and the Café am Neuen See make good stops.

activities.name: BMW Berlin Marathon
activities.description: Watch or run one of the world's six Marathon Majors—a record-breaking flat course that ends at the Brandenburg Gate.
activities.seasonal_notes: Held on the last Sunday of September (27 September in 2026), with cool conditions famous for fast times and world records.
activities.booking_tips: Race entry is by lottery and fills fast; spectating along the route and at the finish is free.

activities.name: Pyronale Fireworks World Championship
activities.description: Watch national teams compete in a dazzling pyromusical fireworks contest staged over the Maifeld at the Olympic Stadium.
activities.seasonal_notes: Held on a late-September weekend (25–26 September in 2026), marking the festival's 20th anniversary.
activities.booking_tips: Buy seated tickets in advance via the Olympiastadion; dress warmly for the evening chill.

activities.name: Berlin Festival of Lights
activities.description: Berlin's landmarks—the Brandenburg Gate, Berlin Cathedral, and more—become canvases for spectacular light-art projections after dark.
activities.seasonal_notes: Runs about ten nights in mid-October (9–18 October in 2026, themed 'Colours of Love'), nightly roughly 7–11pm.
activities.booking_tips: Follow the free walking route between Mitte landmarks; guided light-tour boats and walks can be booked separately.

activities.name: Day of German Unity Celebrations
activities.description: Mark the anniversary of reunification with a big street festival around the Brandenburg Gate and Reichstag—stages, food, and crowds.
activities.seasonal_notes: 3 October is Germany's national holiday; Berlin, as the capital, hosts a multi-day Bürgerfest in the government quarter.
activities.booking_tips: Expect crowds and closed streets near Mitte; travel by U- and S-Bahn.

activities.name: Autumn Walk in the Tiergarten
activities.description: Stroll the winding paths of Berlin's central park as the foliage turns gold, ending at the lakeside Café am Neuen See.
activities.seasonal_notes: October brings peak autumn colour to the Tiergarten and the boulevards of Charlottenburg.
activities.booking_tips: Afternoon light is best for photos; combine with a visit to the nearby Reichstag dome.

activities.name: Berlin Art Week
activities.description: A city-wide celebration of contemporary art with gallery openings, fair previews, museum shows, and special events.
activities.seasonal_notes: Held in mid-September, it opens the new art season as the days cool and the cultural calendar revs up.
activities.booking_tips: Plan a route by district and pre-book any ticketed fair or exhibition entries.

activities.name: Herbstzauber Garden Show at Britzer Garten
activities.description: Explore blazing autumn foliage, dahlias, and seasonal flora across the expansive Britzer Garten during its 'Herbstzauber' season.
activities.seasonal_notes: Autumn colour peaks here, with chrysanthemum and dahlia displays through October.
activities.booking_tips: Wear comfortable shoes—the park is large—and check opening hours, which shorten as the season ends.

activities.name: Museum Island Indoors
activities.description: Spend a cool, grey afternoon among antiquities and Old Masters across the Pergamon, Neues, Bode, and Alte Nationalgalerie.
activities.seasonal_notes: Shorter, cooler autumn days are ideal for museum-hopping on the UNESCO-listed island.
activities.booking_tips: A combined Museum Island ticket saves money; book timed entry online for the busiest collections.

activities.name: Gendarmenmarkt Christmas Market
activities.description: Berlin's most elegant Christmas market sits on its grandest square, ringed by twin cathedrals and the Konzerthaus, with crafts, glühwein, and a stage.
activities.seasonal_notes: Open late November through the end of December (23 Nov–31 Dec in 2026); a small entry fee keeps the crowds manageable.
activities.booking_tips: Visit after dark for the lights and music; weeknights are calmer than weekends.

activities.name: Christmas Market at Charlottenburg Palace
activities.description: Stalls of crafts and seasonal treats glow against the floodlit Baroque facade of Berlin's largest palace—one of the city's prettiest markets.
activities.seasonal_notes: Runs late November to late December (23 Nov–28 Dec in 2026), with the palace as a spectacular backdrop.
activities.booking_tips: Free to enter; come in the early evening when the facade lighting is at its best.

activities.name: Berlin International Film Festival (Berlinale)
activities.description: One of the world's leading film festivals brings premieres, stars, and a packed public programme to cinemas around Potsdamer Platz.
activities.seasonal_notes: The 76th Berlinale runs 12–22 February 2026, the cultural high point of Berlin's winter.
activities.booking_tips: Public tickets go on sale a few days before screenings and sell fast—buy online the moment they open.

activities.name: New Year's Eve at the Brandenburg Gate
activities.description: Ring in the new year at Berlin's famous Silvester party, with live music and a midnight fireworks display at the Brandenburg Gate.
activities.seasonal_notes: The 31 December street party at the gate is among Europe's largest open-air New Year's celebrations.
activities.booking_tips: Arrive early and dress very warmly; the official party zone is ticketed in some years, so check ahead.

activities.name: Ice Skating in the City
activities.description: Lace up at one of Berlin's seasonal outdoor rinks, often set among festive lights and Christmas-market stalls.
activities.seasonal_notes: Pop-up rinks appear from late November through the holidays at markets and squares around the city.
activities.booking_tips: Skate rental is available on site; go on weekday afternoons to avoid the longest queues.

activities.name: Museum Island Galleries
activities.description: Take refuge from the cold among the world-class collections of the Staatliche Museen, from Nefertiti to the Pergamon Altar's antiquities.
activities.seasonal_notes: Grey, freezing winter days are perfect for the warmth and grandeur of Berlin's great museums.
activities.booking_tips: A combined Museum Island ticket or the Museum Pass Berlin offers the best value.

activities.name: Glühwein and Kaffee und Kuchen
activities.description: Warm up the Berlin way—mulled wine at a market or a slice of cake and coffee in a cozy Kreuzberg or Prenzlauer Berg café.
activities.seasonal_notes: Berlin's café culture comes into its own in the dark months, with candlelit rooms and rich winter cakes.
activities.booking_tips: Markets serve glühwein in collectible mugs you can keep for a small deposit.

activities.name: Therme Spa Day
activities.description: Soak away the winter chill at a thermal spa with pools, saunas, and wellness treatments on the city's edge.
activities.seasonal_notes: Saunas and thermal baths are a popular cold-weather escape across Berlin and nearby Brandenburg.
activities.booking_tips: Book treatments ahead and check whether sessions are textile or, as is common in Germany, clothing-optional.
